description: An integer overflow in the bounds check of `TypedArrayPrototypeSetArray` (in `src/builtins/typed-array-set.tq`) allows a V8 sandbox bypass via arbitrary address write. The vulnerability arises because `GetLengthProperty` on a corrupted JSArray can return a `Number` (HeapNumber) whose value exceeds `kMaxSafeInteger`. On 64-bit builds, `ChangeSafeIntegerNumberToUintPtr` and `CheckIntegerIndexAdditionOverflow` only enforce the safe-integer precondition via `dcheck` assertions, which are omitted in release builds. Consequently, if the corrupted source length exceeds `kMaxSafeInteger`, the addition `srcLength + targetOffset` wraps around on 64-bit, causing the bounds check to pass erroneously. Execution then falls through to `Runtime_TypedArraySet` → `CopyElementsHandleImpl` (`src/objects/elements.cc`), where a second integer overflow in `CHECK_LE(offset + length, ...)` can also be bypassed. By additionally performing a type confusion (corrupting a JSArray's map to appear as a JSTypedArray), an attacker can reach `CopyElementsFromTypedArray` with attacker-controlled destination address and content, achieving an arbitrary address wildcopy (out-of-bounds write) outside the V8 sandbox.

fixes.description: Local patch exported from https://crrev.com/c/6285402 for reproducible fixed-image builds.
